creatorDirectoryHiddenStatement: Statement of reasons (DSA Art. 17): community report score {score} reached threshold {threshold} ({n} unique reporters in 14 days), so the in-app public directory no longer shows your tree to others. The tree stays on the network and direct links keep working. Redress: Trees → your row ⋯ → “Contest community reports”, fix the issue and republish, or contact the operator (About → Legal).
creatorLegalDisputeToast: Legal notice on your tree (case {caseId}): you have about 48 hours to publish a signed owner response. Check the bell icon next to your profile. Until then the listing is deprioritized in Discover; without a response, or with two independent legal notices, it is hidden from the in-app index (direct links still work).
creatorModerationAlertLegal: Legal notice on “{title}”. Respond within ~48 h. {case}
creatorModerationAlertThreshold: “{title}” reached the community report threshold ({score}/{threshold}). Hidden from Discover for others.
creatorModerationAlertsLead: Reports and legal notices on trees you published from this device. Restrictions affect Discover only, your links still work.
creatorModerationAlertsPolicyHint: Full moderation rules: About → Legal. Quote the same case reference in e-mail threads with claimants.
publishHubInactivityNote: Public copies auto-retract after about 12 months without use (GDPR). Active learners pause the timer.
creatorReportsToastProgress: Community report score {score} / threshold {threshold} (14 days). Open Trees → your row ⋯ to contest or fix before the listing is hidden.
creatorReportsToast: Your tree received a recent directory report. Check Trees / reports if relevant.
creatorReportsToastMany: Your tree received {n} recent directory reports. Check Trees / reports if relevant.
creatorReportsToastOne: Your tree received a recent directory report. Check Trees / reports if relevant.
creatorUrgentUserMessageToast: Someone left an urgent message for you on this branch (not the app operator). Check “About this tree” if you publish contact there.
curriculumLoadInvalidTreeJson: The branch data is invalid or empty.
curriculumSwitcherTruncBrowseHint: Showing first matches only. Narrow your search.
curriculumSwitcherTruncVersionsHint: Showing first versions only. Narrow your search or open Sources for the full list.
publicTreeCodeAllocFailed: Could not allocate a share code. Try again in a moment.
publicTreeConfirmBody: You are about to publish a full copy of this branch: lessons and minimized progress in the bundle. Shared content is CC BY-SA 4.0 unless you state another compatible license in the material. You can retract later from this device (Construction → Retract). Never include passwords or confidential data.
publicTreeHubConfirmBody: You will publish a copy online. Only share content you are allowed to publish (see About → Legal). Do not include private data.
publicTreeHubRepublishBody: Updates the public copy. Links and share code stay the same.
publicTreeIncludeForumHint: Anyone with the link can read and post in the forum. You can change this when you republish.
publicTreeDockTooltip: Publish a copy online to share it
publicTreePublishComposedTooltip: Publish the full playlist (all branches in this tree)
publishDemoTreeBlocked: The Arborito demo is a shared showcase tree. Duplicate it as your own branch before publishing.
publicTreePublishEventPackageBody:
The app could not build a valid message for the network. This is usually about the branch data or size, not your Wi‑Fi or relays.

You can try:
• Shortening or splitting very large lessons
• Removing odd characters from pasted text (invisible symbols, broken emojis)
• Exporting a backup (.arborito), then updating Arborito and trying again

Technical note (for support): {detail}
publicTreePublishFailedBody:
Something went wrong while publishing online.

Technical detail: {detail}
publicTreePublishForbiddenBody:
The configured relays accepted the connection but rejected your event (typical messages: “blocked”, “restricted”, “only notes signed by the owner”, “not authorized”, or “does not have permission to write”). This is not a Wi‑Fi problem, the relays simply refuse writes from your key.

What you can do:
• Open About this tree → Network health and replace the relays with public ones that accept general writes (avoid “haven” or single-owner private relays).
• If you maintain this Arborito deployment, adjust DEFAULT_NOSTR_RELAYS (docs/NOSTR_RELAYS_CONFIGURATION.md).

Technical detail: {detail}
publicTreePublishNoResult: Publishing did not finish (no public link). Check relays or try again.
publicTreePublishNoResultHint: Often this means no Nostr relay accepted the upload: check your network and that the browser allows secure WebSockets (wss://). If you maintain this Arborito deployment, see docs/NOSTR_RELAYS_CONFIGURATION.md in the repository.
publicTreePublishRelayBody:
Publishing needs at least one working WebSocket relay (wss://). Check your connection or try another network. If you maintain this Arborito deployment, see docs/NOSTR_RELAYS_CONFIGURATION.md in the repository.

Technical detail: {detail}
publicTreePublishTimeout: Publishing took too long (network or peers may be slow). If a loading state is stuck, reload the page, check your connection, and try again.
publicTreePublishWtTimeout: Optional WebTorrent packaging took too long; publishing continues without that layer.
publicTreeRepublishBody: This updates the public copy of this branch on the network (same links and share code). Learner progress in the bundle is still minimized. Continue?
publicTreeRepublishDockTooltip: Review and publish changes to the public copy
publicTreeRepublishSuccessLead: Your public branch was updated. Existing links still work.
publicTreeSuccessCodeHint: Or type it under Trees → Add a tree (no account).
publicTreeSuccessLead: Share the code or link so others can open this course in Arborito.
publicTreeSuccessNoteNoForum: Share the code or link to open the lessons. You can retract the public copy from Construction → Retract.
publicTreeSuccessNote: Share the code or link. You can retract the public copy from Construction → Retract.
treeInactivityAutoRetracted: An unused public copy was retracted automatically (GDPR inactivity policy).
publicTreeSuccessTorrentIndexLag: If you use the optional torrent browse mirror, it refreshes in batches: your tree can take a few minutes to show up there. Share code and links work right away; Nostr search is usually faster.
publicTreeUpdateBranchTooltip: Update the public copy of this branch
publicTreeUpdateComposedTooltip: Update the public playlist (all branches in this tree)
publicTreeUpdateTooltip: Review and publish changes to the public copy
publicTreeUpToDateTooltip: Public copy is up to date
revokePublicTreeConfirmBody: Your tree will stop appearing on the public network. Old links may stop working. Retract?
revokePublicTreeDockTooltip: Retract this tree from the public network (requires publisher key on this device)
revokePublicTreeNoKeyBody: Retracting requires the same browser profile that published this tree. If you cleared storage or use another device, you cannot sign a retraction from here.
revokePublicTreeNoUniverse: Open your own public online branch first.
revokePublicTreeSuccessBody: The public network was asked to stop serving this tree. You can publish again with a new link anytime.
publicTreeListInDiscoverHint: Your share code works the same without this. Discover only lists basic info on the servers you connected.
